config(3) Library Functions Manual config(3)

NAME

config - Config Module


- The toml text file configuration file module.

SYNOPSIS

Files


file config.c
📋 TOML configuration file parser with schema validation and CLI override support file config.h
TOML configuration file support for ascii-chat.

Functions


asciichat_error_t config_load_and_apply (asciichat_mode_t detected_mode, const char *config_path, bool strict, options_t *opts)
Main function to load configuration from file and apply to global options. asciichat_error_t config_load_system_and_user (asciichat_mode_t detected_mode, bool strict, options_t *opts)
Load system config first, then user config (user config overrides system). asciichat_error_t config_create_default (const char *config_path)
Create default configuration file with all default values.

Detailed Description

The toml text file configuration file module.

This module provides functionality for loading configuration from TOML files (typically located at ~/.ascii-chat/config.toml). Configuration values are applied to global options, but command-line arguments always take precedence over config file values.

The interface provides:

  • TOML file parsing and validation
  • Automatic configuration loading from standard location
  • Non-fatal error handling (missing or malformed config files are ignored)
  • Support for network, client, palette, crypto, and logging configuration

Note

Configuration Priority: Command-line arguments override config file values. Config file values override default values. The config file is loaded before CLI argument parsing to ensure this precedence.

Configuration File Location: The config file is loaded from the ascii-chat configuration directory:

  • Unix: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ascii-chat/config.toml if set, otherwise ~/.ascii-chat/config.toml
  • Windows: APPDATA%\ascii-chat\config.toml if set, otherwise ~\.ascii-chat\config.toml

Error Handling: Config file parsing errors are non-fatal. If the file is missing, malformed, or contains invalid values, warnings are printed to stderr and the application continues with default values. Invalid individual values are skipped with warnings, but valid values are still applied.

Validation: All configuration values are validated using the same validation functions used by CLI argument parsing, ensuring consistency between config file and CLI option handling.

Warning

Password Storage: While passwords can be stored in the config file (via crypto.password), this is strongly discouraged for security reasons. A warning is printed if a password is found in the config file. Use CLI --password or environment variables instead.

File Permissions: Users should secure their config file to prevent unauthorized access, especially if it contains sensitive information like encryption keys or passwords.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t config_create_default (const char * config_path)

#include <include/ascii-chat/options/config.h>

Create default configuration file with all default values.

Parameters

config_path Path to config file to create (NULL uses default location)
opts Options structure with values to write (use defaults for a "default" config)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Creates a new configuration file at the specified path (or default location if config_path is NULL) with all configuration options set to values from opts.

The created file includes:

  • Version comment at the top (current ascii-chat version)
  • All supported configuration sections with default values
  • Comments explaining each option

Note

The function will create the directory structure if needed.

If the file already exists, it will not be overwritten (returns error).

Definition at line 1245 of file config.c.

1245                                                                  {
1246   char *config_path_expanded = NULL;
1247 
1248   defer(SAFE_FREE(config_path_expanded));
1249 
1250   // Create fresh options with all OPT_*_DEFAULT values
1251   options_t defaults = options_t_new();
1252 
1253   // Allocate buffer for building config content (256KB should be plenty)
1254   const size_t BUFFER_CAPACITY = 256 * 1024;
1255   config_builder_t builder = {0};
1256   builder.buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(BUFFER_CAPACITY, char *);
1257   if (!builder.buffer) {
1258     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate config buffer");
1259   }
1260   defer(SAFE_FREE(builder.buffer));
1261   builder.capacity = BUFFER_CAPACITY;
1262 
1263   // Build version comment in buffer
1264   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# ascii-chat configuration file\n")) {
1265     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1266   }
1267   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# Generated by ascii-chat v%d.%d.%d-%s\n", ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_MAJOR,
1268                              ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_MINOR, ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_PATCH, ASCII_CHAT_GIT_VERSION)) {
1269     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1270   }
1271   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "#\n")) {
1272     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1273   }
1274   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# All options below are commented out because some configuration options\n")) {
1275     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1276   }
1277   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# conflict with each other (e.g., --file vs --url, --loop vs --url).\n")) {
1278     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1279   }
1280   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# Uncomment only the options you need and avoid conflicting combinations.\n")) {
1281     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1282   }
1283   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "#\n")) {
1284     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1285   }
1286   if (!config_builder_append(&builder,
1287                              "# If you upgrade ascii-chat and this version comment changes, you may need to\n")) {
1288     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1289   }
1290   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# delete and regenerate this file with: ascii-chat --config-create\n")) {
1291     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1292   }
1293   if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "#\n\n")) {
1294     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1295   }
1296 
1297   // Get all options from schema
1298   size_t metadata_count = 0;
1299   const config_option_metadata_t *metadata = config_schema_get_all(&metadata_count);
1300 
1301   // Build list of unique categories in order of first appearance
1302   const char *categories[16] = {0}; // Max expected categories
1303   size_t category_count = 0;
1304 
1305   for (size_t i = 0; i < metadata_count && category_count < 16; i++) {
1306     const char *category = metadata[i].category;
1307     if (!category) {
1308       continue;
1309     }
1310 
1311     // Check if category already in list
1312     bool found = false;
1313     for (size_t j = 0; j < category_count; j++) {
1314       if (categories[j] && strcmp(categories[j], category) == 0) {
1315         found = true;
1316         break;
1317       }
1318     }
1319 
1320     if (!found) {
1321       categories[category_count++] = category;
1322     }
1323   }
1324 
1325   // Build each section dynamically from schema
1326   for (size_t cat_idx = 0; cat_idx < category_count; cat_idx++) {
1327     const char *category = categories[cat_idx];
1328     if (!category) {
1329       continue;
1330     }
1331 
1332     // Get all options for this category
1333     size_t cat_option_count = 0;
1334     const config_option_metadata_t **cat_options = config_schema_get_by_category(category, &cat_option_count);
1335 
1336     if (!cat_options || cat_option_count == 0) {
1337       continue;
1338     }
1339 
1340     // Add section header
1341     if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "[%s]\n", category)) {
1342       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1343     }
1344 
1345     // Track which options we've written (to avoid duplicates)
1346     bool written_flags[64] = {0}; // Max options per category
1347 
1348     // Add each option in this category
1349     for (size_t opt_idx = 0; opt_idx < cat_option_count && opt_idx < 64; opt_idx++) {
1350       const config_option_metadata_t *meta = cat_options[opt_idx];
1351       if (!meta || !meta->toml_key) {
1352         continue;
1353       }
1354 
1355       // Skip if already written (duplicate)
1356       if (written_flags[opt_idx]) {
1357         continue;
1358       }
1359 
1360       // Skip if this is a duplicate of another option (check by TOML key, not field_offset)
1361       // Note: Multiple options can map to the same field (e.g., server_log_file, client_log_file)
1362       bool is_duplicate = false;
1363       for (size_t j = 0; j < opt_idx; j++) {
1364         if (cat_options[j] && cat_options[j]->toml_key && meta->toml_key &&
1365             strcmp(cat_options[j]->toml_key, meta->toml_key) == 0) {
1366           is_duplicate = true;
1367           break;
1368         }
1369       }
1370       if (is_duplicate) {
1371         continue;
1372       }
1373 
1374       // Get field pointer from default options (or mode-specific default if available)
1375       const char *field_ptr = ((const char *)&defaults) + meta->field_offset;
1376 
1377       // Buffer to hold mode-specific default value if needed
1378       char mode_default_buffer[OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE] = {0};
1379       int mode_default_int = 0;
1380 
1381       // If this option has a mode_default_getter, use it to get the correct default
1382       if (meta->mode_default_getter) {
1383         asciichat_mode_t mode = extract_mode_from_bitmask(meta->mode_bitmask);
1384         if (mode != MODE_INVALID) {
1385           const void *default_value = meta->mode_default_getter(mode);
1386           if (default_value) {
1387             // Copy the default value to our buffer based on type
1388             if (meta->type == OPTION_TYPE_STRING || meta->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK) {
1389               const char *str_value = (const char *)default_value;
1390               SAFE_STRNCPY(mode_default_buffer, str_value, sizeof(mode_default_buffer));
1391               field_ptr = mode_default_buffer;
1392             } else if (meta->type == OPTION_TYPE_INT) {
1393               mode_default_int = *(const int *)default_value;
1394               field_ptr = (const char *)&mode_default_int;
1395             }
1396           }
1397         }
1398       }
1399 
1400       // Add description comment if available
1401       if (meta->description && strlen(meta->description) > 0) {
1402         if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# %s\n", meta->description)) {
1403           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1404         }
1405       }
1406 
1407       // Format and add the option value using handler (commented out to avoid conflicts)
1408       if (g_type_handlers[meta->type].format_output) {
1409         char formatted_value[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM] = {0};
1410         g_type_handlers[meta->type].format_output(field_ptr, meta->field_size, meta, formatted_value,
1411                                                   sizeof(formatted_value));
1412         const char *output_key = meta->toml_key;
1413         size_t category_len = strlen(category);
1414         if (strncmp(meta->toml_key, category, category_len) == 0 && meta->toml_key[category_len] == '.') {
1415           output_key = meta->toml_key + category_len + 1; // Strip "<category>."
1416         }
1417 
1418         if (config_key_should_be_commented(meta->toml_key)) {
1419           if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "# %s = %s\n", output_key, formatted_value)) {
1420             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1421           }
1422         } else {
1423           if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "%s = %s\n", output_key, formatted_value)) {
1424             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1425           }
1426         }
1427       }
1428 
1429       // Add blank line after each option
1430       if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "\n")) {
1431         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1432       }
1433 
1434       written_flags[opt_idx] = true;
1435     }
1436 
1437     // Add blank line between sections (but not after the last section)
1438     if (cat_idx < category_count - 1) {
1439       if (!config_builder_append(&builder, "\n")) {
1440         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config too large to fit in buffer");
1441       }
1442     }
1443   }
1444 
1445   // Now write the buffer to either stdout or a file
1446   if (config_path && strlen(config_path) > 0) {
1447     // User provided a filepath - write to that file with overwrite prompt
1448 
1449     // Expand and validate the path
1450     config_path_expanded = expand_path(config_path);
1451     if (!config_path_expanded) {
1452       config_path_expanded = platform_strdup(config_path);
1453     }
1454 
1455     if (!config_path_expanded) {
1456       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to resolve config file path");
1457     }
1458 
1459     char *validated_config_path = NULL;
1460     asciichat_error_t validate_result =
1461         path_validate_user_path(config_path_expanded, PATH_ROLE_CONFIG_FILE, &validated_config_path);
1462     if (validate_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1463       SAFE_FREE(validated_config_path);
1464       SAFE_FREE(config_path_expanded);
1465       return validate_result;
1466     }
1467     // Free the old path before reassigning (defer will free the new one)
1468     if (config_path_expanded != validated_config_path) {
1469       SAFE_FREE(config_path_expanded);
1470     }
1471     config_path_expanded = validated_config_path;
1472 
1473     // Check if file already exists
1474     struct stat st;
1475     if (stat(config_path_expanded, &st) == 0) {
1476       // File exists - ask user if they want to overwrite
1477       log_plain("Config file already exists: %s", config_path_expanded);
1478 
1479       bool overwrite = platform_prompt_yes_no("Overwrite", false); // Default to No
1480       if (!overwrite) {
1481         log_plain("Config file creation cancelled.");
1482         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "User cancelled overwrite");
1483       }
1484 
1485       log_plain("Overwriting existing config file...");
1486     }
1487 
1488     // Create directory if needed
1489     char *dir_path = platform_strdup(config_path_expanded);
1490     if (!dir_path) {
1491       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for directory path");
1492     }
1493     defer(SAFE_FREE(dir_path));
1494 
1495     // Find the last path separator
1496     char *last_sep = strrchr(dir_path, PATH_DELIM);
1497 
1498     if (last_sep) {
1499       *last_sep = '\0';
1500       // Create directory recursively
1501       asciichat_error_t mkdir_result = platform_mkdir_recursive(dir_path, DIR_PERM_PRIVATE);
1502       if (mkdir_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1503         return mkdir_result;
1504       }
1505     }
1506 
1507     // Open file for writing
1508     FILE *output_file = platform_fopen("file_stream", config_path_expanded, "w");
1509     if (!output_file) {
1510       return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open config file for writing: %s", config_path_expanded);
1511     }
1512     defer(SAFE_FCLOSE(output_file));
1513 
1514     // Write buffer to file
1515     size_t written = fwrite(builder.buffer, 1, builder.size, output_file);
1516     if (written != builder.size) {
1517       return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to write config to file: %s", config_path_expanded);
1518     }
1519   } else {
1520     // No filepath provided - write buffer to stdout with automatic retry on transient errors
1521     (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, builder.buffer, builder.size);
1522     // Flush C stdio buffer and terminal to ensure piped output is written immediately
1523     (void)fflush(stdout);
1524     (void)terminal_flush(STDOUT_FILENO);
1525   }
1526 
1527   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1528 }




References ASCII_CHAT_GIT_VERSION,
    ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_MAJOR, ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_MINOR,
    ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_PATCH, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    config_builder_t::buffer, BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM,
    config_builder_t::capacity,
    config_option_metadata_t::category, config_schema_get_all(),
    config_schema_get_by_category(), defer,
    config_option_metadata_t::description, DIR_PERM_PRIVATE,
    ERROR_CONFIG, ERROR_MEMORY, expand_path(),
    config_option_metadata_t::field_offset,
    config_option_metadata_t::field_size, log_plain,
    config_option_metadata_t::mode_bitmask,
    config_option_metadata_t::mode_default_getter, MODE_INVALID,
    OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK, OPTION_TYPE_INT,
    OPTION_TYPE_STRING, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, options_t_new(),
    PATH_DELIM, PATH_ROLE_CONFIG_FILE,
    path_validate_user_path(), platform_fopen(),
    platform_mkdir_recursive(), platform_prompt_yes_no(),
    platform_strdup(), platform_write_all(), SAFE_FCLOSE,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY, SET_ERRNO,
    SET_ERRNO_SYS, config_builder_t::size,
    terminal_flush(), config_option_metadata_t::toml_key, and
    config_option_metadata_t::type.


Referenced by action_create_config(), and
    options_init().






asciichat_error_t config_load_and_apply
  (asciichat_mode_t detected_mode, const char * config_path, bool
  strict, options_t * opts)


#include <lib/options/config/config.c>


Main function to load configuration from file and apply to global
    options. Load configuration from TOML file and apply to global options.




Parameters


is_client true if loading client
  configuration, false for server configuration


config_path Optional path to config file (NULL uses default location)


strict If true, errors are fatal; if false, errors are non-fatal
  warnings


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure (if
  strict) or non-fatal (if !strict)


This is the main entry point for configuration loading. It:



  
1.

  
Expands the config file path (default location or custom path)

  
2.

  
Checks if the file exists and is a regular file

  
3.

  
Parses the TOML file using tomlc17

  
4.

  
Applies configuration from each section (network, client, palette, crypto,
      logging)

  
5.

  
Frees resources and returns




Configuration file errors are non-fatal if strict is false:


    
  
  • Missing file: Returns ASCIICHAT_OK (config file is optional)
    • 
  
  • Not a regular file: Warns and returns ASCIICHAT_OK
    • 
  
  • Parse errors: Warns and returns ASCIICHAT_OK
    • 
  
  • Invalid values: Individual values are skipped with warnings
    • 



If strict is true, any error causes immediate return with error
    code.




Note


This function should be called before
  options_init() parses command-line arguments to ensure CLI arguments
  can override config file values.



Configuration warnings are printed to stderr because logging may
    not be initialized yet when this function is called.




Parameters


is_client true if loading client
  configuration, false for server configuration


config_path Optional path to config file (NULL uses default location)


strict If true, errors are fatal; if false, errors are non-fatal
  warnings


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure (if
  strict) or non-fatal (if !strict)


Loads configuration from the specified path (or default location
    if config_path is NULL) and applies values to global options.




Default config file location (when config_path is NULL):


    
  
  • Unix: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ascii-chat/config.toml if set, otherwise
      ~/.ascii-chat/config.toml
    • 
  
  • Windows: APPDATA%\ascii-chat\config.toml if set, otherwise
      ~.ascii-chat\config.toml
    • 



Only applies configuration values that haven't already been set
    (though in practice, CLI arguments will override config values anyway since
    this is called before CLI parsing).




Supported configuration sections:


    
  
  • [Network Module]: port
    • 
  
  • [server]: bind_ipv4, bind_ipv6
    • 
  
  • [client]: address, width, height, webcam_index, flip_x, flip_y,
      color_mode, render_mode, fps, stretch, quiet, snapshot_mode,
      snapshot_delay, test_pattern, show_capabilities, force_utf8
    • 
  
  • [Audio]: enabled, device
    • 
  
  • [Palette]: type, chars
    • 
  
  • [Crypto Module]: encrypt_enabled, key,
      password, keyfile, no_encrypt, server_key
      (client only), client_keys (server only)
    • 
  
  • [Logging System] or root: log_file
    • 



Note


This function should be called before
  options_init() parses command-line arguments, so that CLI arguments
  can override config file values.



If strict is false and the config file doesn't exist, is not a
    regular file, or fails to parse, the function returns ASCIICHAT_OK
    (non-fatal). Individual invalid values are skipped with warnings, but valid
    values are still applied.




If strict is true, any error (file not found, parse error, etc.)
    causes the function to return an error code immediately.




Warning


Configuration warnings are printed directly to stderr
  because logging may not be initialized yet when this function is called.


Definition at line 1039 of file config.c.


1040                                                          {
1041   // detected_mode is used in config_apply_schema for bitmask validation
1042   char *config_path_expanded = NULL;
1043   defer(SAFE_FREE(config_path_expanded));
1044 
1045   if (config_path) {
1046     // Use custom path provided
1047     config_path_expanded = expand_path(config_path);
1048     if (!config_path_expanded) {
1049       // If expansion fails, try using as-is (might already be absolute)
1050       config_path_expanded = platform_strdup(config_path);
1051     }
1052   } else {
1053     // Use default location with XDG support
1054     char *config_dir = get_config_dir();
1055     defer(SAFE_FREE(config_dir));
1056     if (config_dir) {
1057       size_t len = strlen(config_dir) + strlen("config.toml") + 1;
1058       config_path_expanded = SAFE_MALLOC(len, char *);
1059       if (config_path_expanded) {
1060         safe_snprintf(config_path_expanded, len, "%sconfig.toml", config_dir);
1061       }
1062     }
1063 
1064     // Fallback to ~/.ascii-chat/config.toml
1065     if (!config_path_expanded) {
1066       config_path_expanded = expand_path("~/.ascii-chat/config.toml");
1067     }
1068   }
1069 
1070   if (!config_path_expanded) {
1071     if (strict) {
1072       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to resolve config file path");
1073     }
1074     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1075   }
1076 
1077   char *validated_config_path = NULL;
1078   asciichat_error_t validate_result =
1079       path_validate_user_path(config_path_expanded, PATH_ROLE_CONFIG_FILE, &validated_config_path);
1080   if (validate_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1081     SAFE_FREE(validated_config_path);
1082     SAFE_FREE(config_path_expanded);
1083     return validate_result;
1084   }
1085   // Free the old path before reassigning (defer will free the new one)
1086   if (config_path_expanded != validated_config_path) {
1087     SAFE_FREE(config_path_expanded);
1088   }
1089   config_path_expanded = validated_config_path;
1090 
1091   // Determine display path for error messages (before any early returns)
1092   const char *display_path = config_path ? config_path : config_path_expanded;
1093 
1094   // Log that we're attempting to load config (before logging is initialized, use stderr)
1095   // Only print if terminal output is enabled (suppress with --quiet)
1096   if (config_path && log_get_terminal_output()) {
1097     log_debug("Loading configuration from: %s", display_path);
1098   }
1099 
1100   // Check if config file exists
1101   struct stat st;
1102   if (stat(config_path_expanded, &st) != 0) {
1103     if (strict) {
1104       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config file does not exist: '%s'", display_path);
1105     }
1106     // File doesn't exist, that's OK - not required (non-strict mode)
1107     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1108   }
1109 
1110   // Verify it's a regular file
1111   if (!S_ISREG(st.st_mode)) {
1112     if (strict) {
1113       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Config file exists but is not a regular file: '%s'", display_path);
1114     }
1115     CONFIG_WARN("Config file exists but is not a regular file: '%s' (skipping)", display_path);
1116     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1117   }
1118 
1119   // Parse TOML file
1120   toml_result_t result = toml_parse_file_ex(config_path_expanded);
1121   // Ensure TOML resources are freed at ALL function exit points (defer handles cleanup)
1122   defer(toml_free(result));
1123 
1124   if (!result.ok) {
1125     // result.errmsg is an array, so check its first character
1126     const char *errmsg = (strlen(result.errmsg) > 0) ? result.errmsg : "Unknown parse error";
1127 
1128     if (strict) {
1129       // For strict mode, return detailed error message directly
1130       // Note: SET_ERRNO stores the message in context, but asciichat_error_string() only returns generic codes
1131       // So we need to format the error message ourselves here
1132       char error_buffer[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
1133       safe_snprintf(error_buffer, sizeof(error_buffer), "Failed to parse config file '%s': %s", display_path, errmsg);
1134       toml_free(result); // Explicit cleanup before return (defer transformation not applied)
1135       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "%s", error_buffer);
1136     }
1137     CONFIG_WARN("Failed to parse config file '%s': %s (skipping)", display_path, errmsg);
1138     toml_free(result);   // Explicit cleanup before return (defer transformation not applied)
1139     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Non-fatal error
1140   }
1141 
1142   // Apply configuration using schema-driven parser with bitmask validation
1143   asciichat_error_t schema_result = config_apply_schema(result.toptab, detected_mode, opts, strict);
1144 
1145   if (schema_result != ASCIICHAT_OK && strict) {
1146     toml_free(result); // Explicit cleanup before return (defer transformation not applied)
1147     return schema_result;
1148   }
1149   // In non-strict mode, continue even if some options failed validation
1150 
1151   CONFIG_DEBUG("Loaded configuration from %s", display_path);
1152 
1153   // Log successful config load (use stderr since logging may not be initialized yet)
1154   // Only print if terminal output is enabled (suppress with --quiet)
1155   if (log_get_terminal_output()) {
1156     log_debug("Loaded configuration from: %s", display_path);
1157   }
1158 
1159   // Update RCU system with modified options (for test compatibility)
1160   // In real usage, options_state_set is called later after CLI parsing
1161   asciichat_error_t rcu_result = options_state_set(opts);
1162   if (rcu_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1163     // Non-fatal - RCU might not be initialized yet in some test scenarios
1164     // But log as warning so tests can see if this is the issue
1165     CONFIG_WARN("Failed to update RCU options state: %d (values may not be persisted)", rcu_result);
1166   }
1167 
1168   toml_free(result); // Explicit cleanup before return (defer transformation not applied)
1169   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1170 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM,
    CONFIG_DEBUG, CONFIG_WARN, defer, ERROR_CONFIG,
    expand_path(), get_config_dir(), log_debug,
    log_get_terminal_output(), options_state_set(),
    PATH_ROLE_CONFIG_FILE, path_validate_user_path(),
    platform_strdup(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    safe_snprintf(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by config_load_system_and_user().






asciichat_error_t config_load_system_and_user
  (asciichat_mode_t detected_mode, bool strict, options_t *
  opts)


#include
  <include/ascii-chat/options/config.h>


Load system config first, then user config (user config overrides
    system).


Parameters


detected_mode The detected mode (client or server)


strict If true, user config errors are fatal; system config is always
  non-strict


opts Options structure to write configuration to


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Loads configuration from two locations in order:



  
1.

  
System config: ${INSTALL_PREFIX}/etc/ascii-chat/config.toml (non-strict,
      optional)

  
2.

  
User config: default location (strictness as specified)




User config values override system config values. Both override
    defaults. This function should be called before options_init() parses
    command-line arguments.




Note


System config is always loaded non-strict (missing file
  is not an error)



User config strictness follows the strict parameter




Definition at line 1530 of file config.c.


1530                                                                                                             {
1531   // Use platform abstraction to find all config.toml files across standard locations
1532   config_file_list_t config_files = {0};
1533   asciichat_error_t search_result = platform_find_config_file("config.toml", &config_files);
1534   defer(config_file_list_destroy(&config_files));
1535 
1536   if (search_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1537     CONFIG_DEBUG("Failed to search for config files: %d", search_result);
1538     config_file_list_destroy(&config_files);
1539     return search_result;
1540   }
1541 
1542   // Cascade load: Load all found configs in reverse order (lowest priority first)
1543   // This allows higher-priority configs to override lower-priority values.
1544   // Example: System configs load first, then user configs override them.
1545 
1546   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
1547   for (size_t i = config_files.count; i > 0; i--) {
1548     const config_file_result_t *file = &config_files.files[i - 1];
1549 
1550     // Determine strictness based on whether this is a system or user config
1551     // System configs are non-strict (values can be missing, errors are non-fatal)
1552     // User config is strict or non-strict based on parameter
1553     bool is_user_config = !file->is_system_config;
1554     bool file_strict = is_user_config ? strict : false;
1555 
1556     CONFIG_DEBUG("Loading config from %s (system=%s, strict=%s)", file->path, file->is_system_config ? "yes" : "no",
1557                  file_strict ? "true" : "false");
1558 
1559     asciichat_error_t load_result = config_load_and_apply(detected_mode, file->path, file_strict, opts);
1560 
1561     if (load_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1562       if (file_strict) {
1563         // Strict mode: errors are fatal
1564         CONFIG_DEBUG("Strict config loading failed for %s", file->path);
1565         result = load_result;
1566       } else {
1567         // Non-strict mode: errors are non-fatal, just log and continue
1568         CONFIG_DEBUG("Non-strict config loading warning for %s: %d (continuing)", file->path, load_result);
1569         CLEAR_ERRNO(); // Clear error context for next file
1570       }
1571     }
1572   }
1573 
1574   config_file_list_destroy(&config_files);
1575   return result;
1576 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, CLEAR_ERRNO,
    CONFIG_DEBUG, config_file_list_destroy(),
    config_load_and_apply(), config_file_list_t::count,
    defer, config_file_list_t::files,
    config_file_result_t::is_system_config,
    config_file_result_t::path, and
  platform_find_config_file().


Referenced by options_init().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.11.2
    ascii-chat
